page: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v7 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Daldsv7 server family. Powered by an AMD processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 8 dedicated vCPUs and is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core.

This server type includes 472 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It does not include any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware configuration represents a low-memory profile relative to the vCPU count, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.

The low-memory architecture provides a cost-efficient option for workloads that require dedicated compute resources but do not demand large memory footprints. This resource balance makes the instance suitable for compute-focused tasks, batch processing, web serving, and applications utilizing local scratch space, while serving as a less optimal choice for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data analytics.
description: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Daldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor, paired with 16.0 GB of system memory, which establishes a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance is equipped with 472 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is designed for compute-focused workloads that do not require extensive memory capacity, offering a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for resource-constrained budgets. It is suitable for tasks such as web serving, batch processing, and lightweight application hosting where local storage is beneficial.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v7 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine from the Daldsv7 family featuring an AMD processor, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 472 GB of local storage for compute-focused workloads.

tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine featuring dedicated compute cores, low memory density, and local storage for cost-efficient processing workloads.
